heading: Preliminary determinations
label: 1673b Preliminary determinations
notes: Amendments1994—Subsec. (a). , amended heading and text of subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Except in the case of a petition dismissed by the administering authority under , the Commission, within 45 days after the date on which a petition is filed under or on which it receives notice from the administering authority of an investigation commenced under , shall make a determination, based upon the best information available to it at the time of the determination, of whether there is a reasonable indication that—Pub. L. 103–465, § 212(b)(2)(A)section 1673a(c)(3) of this titlesection 1673a(b) of this titlesection 1673a(a) of this title“(1) an industry in the United States—“(A) is materially injured, or“(B) is threatened with material injury, or“(2) the establishment of an industry in the United States is materially retarded,by reason of imports of the merchandise which is the subject of the investigation by the administering authority. If that determination is negative, the investigation shall be terminated.”Subsec. (1) read as follows: “Within 160 days after the date on which a petition is filed under , or an investigation is commenced under , but not before an affirmative determination by the Commission under subsection (a) of this section, the administering authority shall make a determination, based upon the best information available to it at the time of the determination, of whether there is a reasonable basis to believe or suspect that the merchandise is being sold, or is likely to be sold at less than fair value. children.heading: Determination by Commission of reasonable indication of injury
children.label: a Determination by Commission of reasonable indication of injury
i
children.text: is materially injured, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is threatened with material injury, or

children.label: B
children.text: the establishment of an industry in the United States is materially retarded,

children.label: i
children.text: within 45 days after the date on which the petition is filed, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the time has been extended pursuant to , within 25 days after the date on which the Commission receives notice from the administering authority of initiation of the investigation, andsection 1673a(c)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an investigation initiated under , within 45 days after the date on which the Commission receives notice from the administering authority that an investigation has been initiated under such section.section 1673a(a) of this title

children.heading: Preliminary determination by administering authority
children.label: b Preliminary determination by administering authority

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), within 140 days after the date on which the administering authority initiates an investigation under , or an investigation is initiated under , but not before an affirmative determination by the Commission under subsection (a) of this section, the administering authority shall make a determination, based upon the information available to it at the time of the determination, of whether there is a reasonable basis to believe or suspect that the merchandise is being sold, or is likely to be sold, at less than fair value.section 1673a(c) of this titlesection 1673a(a) of this title

children.heading: If certain short life cycle merchandise involved
children.label: B If certain short life cycle merchandise involved
i
children.text: by substituting “100 days” for “140 days” if manufacturers that are second offenders account for a significant proportion of the merchandise under investigation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: by substituting “80 days” for “140 days” if manufacturers that are multiple offenders account for a significant proportion of the merchandise under investigation.

children.label: I
children.text: specified in both such determinations, and

children.label: II
children.text: within the scope of the product category referred to in subparagraph (B).

children.label: I
children.text: specified in each of such determinations, and

children.label: II
children.text: within the scope of the product category referred to in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Preliminary determination under waiver of verification
children.label: 2 Preliminary determination under waiver of verification
children.text: Within 75 days after the initiation of an investigation, the administering authority shall cause an official designated for such purpose to review the information concerning the case received during the first 60 days of the investigation, and, if there appears to be sufficient information available upon which the preliminary determination can reasonably be based, to disclose to the petitioner and any interested party, then a party to the proceedings that requests such disclosure, all available nonconfidential information and all other information which is disclosed pursuant to . Within 3 days (not counting Saturdays, Sundays, or legal public holidays) after such disclosure, the petitioner and each party which is an interested party described in subparagraph (C), (D), (E), (F), or (G) of to whom such disclosure was made may furnish to the administering authority an irrevocable written waiver of verification of the information received by the authority, and an agreement that it is willing to have a preliminary determination made on the basis of the record then available to the authority. If a timely waiver and agreement have been received from the petitioner and each party which is an interested party described in subparagraph (C), (D), (E), (F), or (G) of to whom the disclosure was made, and the authority finds that sufficient information is then available upon which the preliminary determination can reasonably be based, a preliminary determination shall be made within 90 days after the initiation of the investigation on the basis of the record established during the first 60 days after the investigation was initiated.section 1677f of this titlesection 1677(9) of this titlesection 1677(9) of this title

children.heading: De minimis dumping margin
children.label: 3 De minimis dumping margin
children.text: In making a determination under this subsection, the administering authority shall disregard any weighted average dumping margin that is de minimis. For purposes of the preceding sentence, a weighted average dumping margin is de minimis if the administering authority determines that it is less than 2 percent ad valorem or the equivalent specific rate for the subject merchandise.

children.heading: Extension of period in extraordinarily complicated cases
children.label: c Extension of period in extraordinarily complicated cases
A
children.text: the petitioner makes a timely request for an extension of the period within which the determination must be made under subsection (b)(1), or

children.label: I
children.text: the number and complexity of the transactions to be investigated or adjustments to be considered,

children.label: II
children.text: the novelty of the issues presented, or

children.label: III
children.text: the number of firms whose activities must be investigated, and

children.label: ii
children.text: additional time is necessary to make the preliminary determination,

children.heading: Notice of postponement
children.label: 2 Notice of postponement
children.text: The administering authority shall notify the parties to the investigation, not later than 20 days before the date on which the preliminary determination would otherwise be required under subsection (b)(1), if it intends to postpone making the preliminary determination under paragraph (1). The notification shall include an explanation of the reasons for the postponement, and notice of the postponement shall be published in the Federal Register.

children.heading: Effect of determination by the administering authority
children.label: d Effect of determination by the administering authority
i
children.text: determine an estimated weighted average dumping margin for each exporter and producer individually investigated, and

children.label: ii
children.text: determine, in accordance with , an estimated all-others rate for all exporters and producers not individually investigated, andsection 1673d(c)(5) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: shall order the posting of a cash deposit, bond, or other security, as the administering authority deems appropriate, for each entry of the subject merchandise in an amount based on the estimated weighted average dumping margin or the estimated all-others rate, whichever is applicable,

children.label: A
children.text: the date on which notice of the determination is published in the Federal Register, or

children.label: B
children.text: the date that is 60 days after the date on which notice of the determination to initiate the investigation is published in the Federal Register, and

shall make available to the Commission all information upon which such determination was based and which the Commission considers relevant to its injury determination, under such procedures as the administering authority and the Commission may establish to prevent disclosure, other than with the consent of the party providing it or under protective order, of any information to which confidential treatment has been given by the administering authority.

children.label: i
children.text: there is a history of dumping and material injury by reason of dumped imports in the United States or elsewhere of the subject merchandise, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the person by whom, or for whose account, the merchandise was imported knew or should have known that the exporter was selling the subject merchandise at less than its fair value and that there was likely to be material injury by reason of such sales, and

children.label: B
children.text: there have been massive imports of the subject merchandise over a relatively short period.

children.label: A
children.text: the date which is 90 days before the date on which the suspension of liquidation was first ordered, or

children.label: B
children.text: the date on which notice of the determination to initiate the investigation is published in the Federal Register.

children.heading: Notice of determination
children.label: f Notice of determination
children.text: Whenever the Commission or the administering authority makes a determination under this section, the Commission or the administering authority, as the case may be, shall notify the petitioner, and other parties to the investigation, and the Commission or the administering authority (whichever is appropriate) of its determination. The administering authority shall include with such notification the facts and conclusions on which its determination is based. Not later than 5 days after the date on which the determination is required to be made under subsection (a)(2), the Commission shall transmit to the administering authority the facts and conclusions on which its determination is based.
